ambiguities: cos-lending-selling-dags references 'Vampire system' for bidirectional data exchange but Vampire's purpose and API contract are undefined
cos-lending-selling-ingestion references 'Vampire DB' requiring data export/import but the system's role in loan lifecycle is unclear
Cos.Lending.Selling.Contracts defines 'field1' through 'field21' properties on LoanInfo with no business meaning documented
COS.Lending.Selling.WebApi references 'GroomingRecovery' process but implementation details and business rules are not defined
cos-lending-selling-ingestion uses loan 'status >= 5' threshold for sync but status code meanings are not documented
Cos.Lending.Selling.Contracts shows issuingBankId vs servicingBankId relationship but their distinct roles are unclear
COS.Lending.Selling.WebApi mentions auto-purchase eligibility criteria but specific rules are not documented
systemOverview: Cross River Bank's Loan Selling and Servicing Platform is a multi-tenant system that enables marketplace lenders (MPLs) to sell consumer loans to investors through the bank. The system manages the complete loan lifecycle from origination through purchase, servicing, interest accrual, fee collection, and reporting. It provides web interfaces for loan management, AI-powered chat for loan history queries, automated batch processing workflows, and integrations with CRB's core banking system (COS). The platform handles financial operations including transfers, interest calculations using SOFR rates, volume fees, and reconciliation between multiple systems.

serviceMap.role: React frontend providing loan inventory management, filtering, pending approvals, and AI chat interface
.NET backend API managing loan operations, purchases, transfers, interest accrual, fee processing, and batch operations
Event notification service publishing loan selling events to hooks hub
Shared contract library defining data types and entities used across services
Database schema and Entity Framework context for PostgreSQL persistence layer
Python AI service providing natural language querying of loan data using AWS Bedrock
Airflow DAGs orchestrating ETL workflows for loan data ingestion, interest accrual, fee collection, and reporting
Python ETL service ingesting servicing data from MPLs via S3, synchronizing loan status, importing SOFR rates
.NET utility resolving and mapping accounts for loans from accounting and operations databases
dbt-based data warehouse providing analytics transformations for loans, contracts, fees, and volume data
End-to-end testing framework simulating complete loan lifecycle with mocked external services
Terraform/Terragrunt infrastructure provisioning AWS resources (Aurora, MWAA, ECS, Lambda, SQS)

features.name: Loan Purchase and Selling
features.description: End-to-end workflow for MPLs to sell loans to investors through batch processing
features.dataFlow: User selects loans in UI → WebApi creates batch → Outbox processor initiates transfer requests → COS Transaction Service processes transfers → Batch completion triggers hooks notification → Status updated in DB

features.name: Interest Accrual
features.description: Daily interest calculation on loans based on SOFR rates and contract terms
features.dataFlow: Ingestion fetches SOFR rates from Federal Reserve → Airflow DAG triggers daily interest job → WebApi calculates interest per loan using DailyInterestOutbox → Interest posted to accounting → History recorded in InterestHistory table

features.name: Volume Fee Processing
features.description: Monthly minimum fee calculations and true-up adjustments for MPLs
features.dataFlow: Airflow triggers volume fee DAG → WebApi calculates fees against monthly minimums via VolumeFeeOutbox → True-up charges calculated via TrueUpVolumeFeeOutbox → Fees collected via accounting → Hooks publishes TrueUpVolumeFeeCharged event

features.name: Loan Servicing Data Sync
features.description: Regular ingestion of servicing updates from MPLs to track loan status and payments
features.dataFlow: MPLs upload CSV files to S3 → Airflow triggers servicing DAG → Ingestion service reads S3 files → Servicing values updated in Selling DB → Timestamps recorded per MPL → Status synchronized from Arix DB

features.name: AI Loan History Chat
features.description: Natural language interface for querying loan history, interest, and fees
features.dataFlow: User asks question in UI → UI creates/resumes chat session → AI service queries loan DB using AWS Bedrock LLM → SQL generated and executed → Results formatted as natural language → Streamed via SSE to UI → Chat history stored in DynamoDB

features.name: Loan Grooming and Type Changes
features.description: Changing loan types and investor assignments after seasoning periods
features.dataFlow: Seasoning period completed → WebApi grooming process changes loan type → Account mappings updated via data-utils → LoanTypeChanged event published via hooks → Investor assignments updated if applicable

features.name: Batch Reporting
features.description: Pre-sale and post-sale loan reports generation for compliance and operations
features.dataFlow: Report request submitted via API → ReportingOutbox queues generation → Report data extracted from DB → Data transformed via dbt models → CSV/Excel generated → Uploaded to S3 storage → Download link returned

features.name: Auto Purchase Flow
features.description: Automated loan purchasing based on contract eligibility criteria
features.dataFlow: New loans ingested → Eligibility evaluated against contract terms → Auto-purchase flag triggers batch creation → Batch initialization via BatchInitOutbox → Purchase processed automatically → Completion notification sent

dataFlows.name: Loan Origination to Purchase
dataFlows.description: Complete flow from loan creation through purchase by MPL
steps: Loan originated in external system and ingested via cos-lending-selling-ingestion
Loan data synchronized to Selling DB from Arix
Account mappings resolved via cos-lending-selling-data-utils from Accounting API
Loan appears in COS.Lending.Selling.UI inventory
MPL selects loans and creates batch via WebApi
WebApi validates loan eligibility and creates batch record
BatchInitOutbox processes batch and initiates transfers
TransferOutbox creates transfer requests to COS Transaction Service
COS processes transfers between accounts
Batch status updated to completed
Hooks service publishes BatchPurchaseCompleted event
Loan ownership transferred to MPL

dataFlows.name: Daily Interest Accrual
dataFlows.description: Automated daily interest calculation and posting
steps: Airflow DAG triggers at scheduled time
cos-lending-selling-ingestion ensures latest SOFR rates available
WebApi DailyInterestOutbox processor runs
For each active loan, calculate interest based on contract terms and SOFR
Interest amount calculated using loan balance and rate
Interest posted to accounting system via COS.Lending.Accounting API
InterestHistory record created in DB
Transfer created for interest payment
Metrics published to CloudWatch
Completion logged

dataFlows.name: Servicing Data Pipeline
dataFlows.description: MPL servicing data ingestion and processing
steps: MPL uploads CSV servicing file to designated S3 bucket
Airflow servicing DAG detects new file
cos-lending-selling-ingestion downloads and parses CSV
Servicing values (adjusted_loan_amount, interest_paid) extracted
Data validated against loan records in DB
Servicing table updated with new values
Servicing timestamp recorded for MPL
File moved to processed folder in S3
Metrics updated for monitoring dashboard
dbt models refresh analytics tables

dataFlows.name: End-to-End Loan Query via AI
dataFlows.description: User question answered by AI service with database context
steps: User enters question in COS.Lending.Selling.UI chat interface
UI calls cos-lending-selling-ai /chat endpoint with user_id and loan_id
AI service creates or retrieves session from DynamoDB
User message posted to /chat/{session_id}/message
AI service constructs prompt with loan schema and history
AWS Bedrock (Claude) LLM processes question
If data needed, AI generates SQL query
SQL executed against Selling DB via SQLAlchemy
Results formatted into natural language response
Response streamed via SSE to UI
Message history persisted to DynamoDB
UI displays formatted response with citations

dataModel.description: Core entity representing consumer loan with loan_id, loan_number, loan_type, loan_amount, interest_rate, status, mpl_id, issuing_bank_id, servicing_bank_id, investor_id, origination_date, maturity_date
Agreement between MPL and bank defining purchase terms, fees, interest calculation methods, seasoning periods, volume minimums
Group of loans processed together with batch_id, status, creation_date, mpl_id, total_amount, loan_count, progress statistics
Financial transaction between accounts with transfer_id, amount, from_account, to_account, status, type, loan_id, created_date
Audit trail of actions on loans (purchase, interest_accrual, fee_collection) with action_type, amount, timestamp, user_id
Interest calculations over time with interest_date, principal_balance, interest_rate, sofr_rate, interest_amount, loan_id
Fee structures and charges with fee_type, amount, percentage, frequency, mpl_id, contract_id, collection_date
Banking accounts for transfers with account_number, account_type, is_subaccount, objective (purchase/return/interest/fee), mpl_id
Partner organizations purchasing loans with mpl_id, name, enabled status, contract terms
Organizations investing in loan portfolios with investor_id, name, allocation rules
Financial institutions (issuing or servicing) with bank_id, name, type
Secured Overnight Financing Rate with rate_date, rate_value, 30day_average from Federal Reserve
MPL-provided loan servicing data with adjusted_loan_amount, interest_paid, last_payment_date, servicing_timestamp
AI chat conversation with session_id, user_id, loan_id, created_date, last_activity
Monthly fee minimums per MPL with month, year, minimum_amount, actual_amount, true_up_amount

architecturePatterns.description: Reliable event publishing using transactional outbox tables for transfers, fees, batches, interest, notifications, and reporting
Asynchronous communication via hooks, SQS queues, and NServiceBus for loan events, status changes, and financial operations
Tenant isolation for MPLs, Banks, and Investors with tenant-specific data access and authorization
Separation of command operations (purchases, transfers) from query operations (reporting, analytics)
Data access abstraction layer separating business logic from persistence
Extract-Transform-Load workflows orchestrated by Airflow with dbt transformations
F# business logic with pure functions wrapped by C# imperative API layer
Independently deployable services with clear boundaries: UI, API, AI, Hooks, Ingestion, Data Utils
FastAPI serving as gateway for AI requests with authentication, streaming, and rate limiting
Docker containers for all services deployed on AWS ECS Fargate
Terraform and Terragrunt managing AWS resources with multi-environment configuration
